title: Connection
description: A first-class, non-secret description of how to reach a system: address here, credentials in the SecretReference named by spec.secretRef. Managed connections are realized by a connection-capable Provider as a stable platform-owned entrypoint (a forwarder on the shared network and the host) whose target is where the system actually lives; external connections are plain address records consumed as-is. External resources' connectionRef resolves to a Connection (preferred) or directly to a SecretReference (the v1.0.0 shorthand).

providerRef.description: The connection-capable Provider realizing the entrypoint. Required unless external.

external.description: A plain address record; nothing is created for it.

scheme.description: Transport scheme; the realizing provider must declare it in SupportedConnectionSchemes(). Default: tcp.

host.description: External only: where the system answers.

port.description: The port consumers use. Managed: the entrypoint's listen port on the shared network and the host.

target.description: Managed only: host:port the entrypoint forwards to — the one place that knows where the system actually lives.

secretRef.description: Optional SecretReference carrying credentials for whatever answers at this connection.

tls.description: On a managed (non-external) Connection with scheme: https, terminates TLS at the entrypoint (docs/planning/08 C8) — exactly one of secretRef, selfSigned, or secretName. On an external Connection, declares the outbound TLS posture used to reach a TLS-requiring database (docs/planning/08 I2, docs/adr/025) via mode (required) and an optional caSecretRef; absent spec.tls on an external Connection preserves the historical plaintext behavior.

secretRef.description: Managed only: SecretReference carrying the cert+key PEM material (keys: cert, key). Must also appear in the realizing Provider's spec.secretRefs for the engine to resolve it (mirrors spec.secretRef's own plumbing).

selfSigned.description: Managed only: the provider provisions a local CA plus a per-host leaf certificate for dev use. The CA's public certificate is published in providerState so tools can trust it.

secretName.description: Managed only, Kubernetes only: references an existing kubernetes.io/tls Secret by name (e.g. cert-manager-managed). platformctl only ever reads this Secret.

mode.description: External only: the outbound TLS posture used to reach the database. Required when spec.tls is declared on an external Connection.

caSecretRef.description: External only: optional SecretReference holding a CA bundle PEM under key "ca" (e.g. an RDS/private CA), used to verify the server certificate under mode verify-ca/verify-full. Must also appear in the consuming Provider's spec.secretRefs for the engine to resolve it.

via.description: Managed only: optional reference to a tunnel-capable Provider (docs/adr/023) this Connection's egress additionally routes through. Schema-accepted and validate-time capability-checked (the named Provider must implement TunnelCapableProvider); no realizing provider consumes it yet — a tunnel-mediated Connection is realized directly by the tunnel provider itself today (see docs/adr/002's addendum, docs/adr/023's Scope section).
